rationale.claude: Editorial bar for cover letters. The opening sentence ('Digitech keeps EMS billing moving by protecting compliance and keeping collections on track') demonstrates real understanding of what the company actually does — not just 'I see you do healthcare' but the specific operational mission (EMS billing → compliance + collections). Every paragraph makes a specific move: para 2 = concrete BTB workflow with RustDesk; para 3 = the 'documentation as repeat-fix-prevention' habit with a specific metric; para 4 = remote-work + after-hours + the military-records-discipline transferable skill. No AI-tells, no boilerplate. Closing 'help Digitech keep service steady and compliance protected' echoes the opening's mission framing — circular structure done right. Dates display cleanly, no overclaims.

key_strengths: Opening sentence demonstrates research — names Digitech's actual business mechanism (EMS billing → compliance + collections), not generic flattery

Para 2 follows the candidate through one ticket end-to-end ('assessed each request, gathered the details that mattered, documented the actions taken in our ticketing system, using RustDesk for remote support, and resolving Windows, TCP/IP, and DNS problems that were keeping users from working') — reader can imagine the candidate working

Escalation handling specifically described: 'When an issue sat outside my lane, I escalated it with the context already attached so it could be picked up without backtracking' — this is what hiring managers actually want to read
Military service mentioned as transferable-skills bridge ('the discipline I built in regulated environments during my U.S. Army Reserve service') without inflating to 'extensive HIPAA training'
Closing references both the company's mission ('service steady and compliance protected') AND the candidate's specific contribution domain
key_weaknesses: Could open paragraph 4 ('I am also comfortable...') with a less formulaic transition phrase — 'I am also' reads slightly stiff
specific_fixes: Rewrite paragraph 4 opener as 'For the remote and after-hours pieces of the role: I handle that work independently...' or similar — minor polish
tier_definition_match: Matches target (~9.4): the editorial decisions are visible and confident. The opening hook is researched, every paragraph has a distinct purpose, no AI-tells, military service is used as a transferable skill (not inflated), and the circular structure ties back to the company's mission. This is what cover letters from the pipeline should aspire to.
